frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: standard RL update rules suffice to learn log-likelihood ratios.

frameworks.name: log-likelihood ratio accumulation
frameworks.one_line: evidence integration mechanism shown to emerge from RL value learning.

frameworks.name: dedicated synaptic plasticity rules
frameworks.one_line: prior models requiring bespoke plasticity are argued unnecessary.

core_question: Can neural circuits learn to accumulate probabilistic evidence using only standard reinforcement learning rules rather than dedicated plasticity?
key_assumptions: binary-choice decisions integrate log-likelihood ratios of sensory inputs
for a wide task class, log-likelihood ratios are approximately linear in expected reward
synaptic updates follow standard RL value-learning dynamics
manipulations: task class with probabilistic sensory cues
comparison of standard RL plasticity vs. specialized log-likelihood plasticity rules
key_findings: RL-trained circuits estimate log-likelihood ratios from experience
trial-by-trial accumulation of cue-associated log-likelihood ratios emerges during action selection
model replicates behavioral and neural data from probabilistic-cue tasks
interpretations: dedicated plasticity rules for probabilistic inference are not required
standard RL mechanisms suffice for efficient probabilistic inference in neural circuits
